# aarambh-ai v1.0.0

aarambh-ai v1.0.0 is the first production source release of the Rust LLM workspace. It includes the complete 14-crate stack from tokenizer and data loading through transformer training, inference, quantisation, fine-tuning, GRPO, safety, self-learning, GPU scale-up hooks, and custom CPU/CUDA kernel paths.

This release is source-first:

- No crates are published to crates.io in v1.0.0.
- No pretrained checkpoints, model weights, adapters, or GGUF artifacts are attached to this release.
- Users build the CLI from source and train, fine-tune, quantise, or convert their own checkpoints.
- CUDA support remains optional and must be validated on a CUDA host with NVCC.

## Highlights

- Decoder-only transformer with Tiny, Small, Medium, and Large model configurations.
- BPE tokenizer with fixed project special-token IDs for EOS, padding, chat, and thinking markers.
- Data pipeline for plaintext and JSONL records.
- Neural network stack with RMSNorm, RoPE, grouped-query attention, SwiGLU, transformer blocks, KV cache, and tied LM head support.
- Full training loop with AdamW, cosine warmup/decay, gradient clipping, checkpoint save/resume, evaluation, and throughput logging.
- Inference CLI with greedy/top-k/top-p sampling, streaming, predict-view, KV cache decode, context-limit handling, and thinking modes.
- Quantisation stack with INT8, GPTQ INT4, AWQ INT4, GGUF Q4_K_M/Q5_K_M/Q8_0 helpers, QAT primitives, and INT8 KV-cache storage.
- Fine-tuning stack with LoRA, QLoRA, SFT, adapter persistence, adapter merge, and thinking SFT formatting.
- GRPO reinforcement learning with deterministic math/format verifiers, reference KL, grouped rollout sampling, and adapter-only optimization.
- Safety layer with input prompt-injection/jailbreak checks, PII detection/redaction, toxicity checks, policy presets, and privacy-safe audit logs.
- Self-learning loop with online GRPO, replay buffer, critique, persistent LoRA state, CPU-deferred gradients, and explicit management commands.
- GPU scale-up support with opt-in CUDA feature forwarding, BF16/F16 dtype-aware train/infer paths, WikiText-103 configs, Kaggle notebooks, and packaging scripts.
- Custom kernel path with stable CPU SIMD RMSNorm, rayon parallel attention, Flash Attention CUDA PTX loading, fused RMSNorm, fused RoPE, and fused SwiGLU fallbacks.
- Strict public API documentation with `#![deny(missing_docs)]` across library crates.
- CI and release workflows for CPU/default validation, docs, release build, and CLI smoke tests.

## Build From Source

```sh
git clone https://github.com/AarambhDevHub/aarambh-ai.git
cd aarambh-ai
git checkout v1.0.0
cargo build --release -p aarambh-ai
```

Optional local install from the checked-out source tree:

```sh
cargo install --path aarambh-ai
aarambh-ai --version
aarambh-ai --help
```

Expected version output:

```text
aarambh-ai 1.0.0
```

## CPU Validation

```sh
cargo fmt --check
cargo check --workspace
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test --workspace
RUSTDOCFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo doc --workspace --no-deps
cargo build --release -p aarambh-ai
```

CLI smoke checks:

```sh
cargo run -p aarambh-ai -- --version
cargo run -p aarambh-ai -- --help
cargo run -p aarambh-ai -- train --help
cargo run -p aarambh-ai -- infer --help
cargo run -p aarambh-ai -- quantise --help
cargo run -p aarambh-ai -- convert --help
cargo run -p aarambh-ai -- finetune --help
cargo run -p aarambh-ai -- selflearn --help
```

## Train And Run Your Own Checkpoint

Tiny Shakespeare remains the fastest local smoke path once `data/tiny_shakespeare.txt` exists:

```sh
cargo run --release -p aarambh-ai -- train --config configs/tiny_shakespeare_smoke.toml
```

Inference from a user-created checkpoint:

```sh
cargo run --release -p aarambh-ai -- infer \
  --config configs/tiny_shakespeare.toml \
  --model checkpoints/tiny_shakespeare/best/model.safetensors \
  --tokenizer checkpoints/tiny_shakespeare/tokenizer.json \
  --prompt "To be, or not to be" \
  --max-tokens 64 \
  --greedy
```

## CUDA Notes

Default builds do not require CUDA or NVCC. CUDA kernels are optional:

```sh
cargo run --release -p aarambh-ai --features cuda -- train \
  --config configs/wikitext103_cuda_smoke.toml
```

Run CUDA validation on a CUDA host only. If NVCC is not installed, the kernel crate emits a warning and keeps the CPU/Candle fallback path enabled.

## Release Policy

v1.0.0 intentionally does not publish crates to crates.io and does not ship pretrained checkpoints. Future releases may add binary artifacts, hosted model weights, or registry publishing after the source release process is stable.
